heading: Indian Health Service and Department of Veterans Affairs health facilities and services sharing
label: 1680f Indian Health Service and Department of Veterans Affairs health facilities and services sharing

L. 108–170, title II, § 244117 Stat. 2054Dec. 6, 2003“The Department of Veterans Affairs Medical Center in Salt Lake City, Utah, shall after the date of the enactment of this Act [] be known and designated as the ‘George E. Wahlen Department of Veterans Affairs Medical Center’. Any references to such facility in any law, regulation, map, document, record, or other paper of the United States shall be considered to be a reference to the George E. Wahlen Department of Veterans Affairs Medical Center.”Dec. 6, 2003

children.heading: Feasibility study and report
children.label: a Feasibility study and report
children.text: The Secretary shall examine the feasibility of entering into an arrangement for the sharing of medical facilities and services between the Indian Health Service and the Department of Veterans Affairs and shall, in accordance with subsection (b), prepare a report on the feasibility of such an arrangement and submit such report to the Congress by no later than .September 30, 1990

children.heading: Nonimpairment of service quality, eligibility, or priority of access
children.label: b Nonimpairment of service quality, eligibility, or priority of access

children.text: the priority access of any Indian to health care services provided through the Indian Health Service;
the quality of health care services provided to any Indian through the Indian Health Service;
the priority access of any veteran to health care services provided by the Department of Veterans Affairs;
the quality of health care services provided to any veteran by the Department of Veterans Affairs;
the eligibility of any Indian to receive health services through the Indian Health Service; or
the eligibility of any Indian who is a veteran to receive health services through the Department of Veterans Affairs.

children.label: A
children.text: individuals in the vicinity of Roosevelt, Utah, who are eligible for health care from the Department of Veterans Affairs could obtain health care services at the facilities of the Indian Health Service located at Fort Duchesne, Utah; and

children.label: B
children.text: individuals eligible for health care from the Indian Health Service at Fort Duchesne, Utah, could obtain health care services at the George E. Wahlen Department of Veterans Affairs Medical Center located in Salt Lake City, Utah.
Not later than , the Secretary and the Secretary of Veterans Affairs shall jointly submit a report to the Congress on the health care services provided as a result of paragraph (1).November 23, 1990

children.heading: Right to health services
children.label: d Right to health services
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed as creating any right of a veteran to obtain health services from the Indian Health Service except as provided in an agreement under subsection (c).
